children.text: • ⬆ Muerte de motoneuronas superiores e inferiores.
• Resultado: parálisis muscular progresiva ➡ insuficiencia respiratoria ➡ muerte.
• Función cognitiva (en la mayoría de los casos)
• Factores de riesgo: ♂ edad avanzada, exposiciones ambientales.
1. Hardiman, O., et al. “Amyotrophic\Nlateral sclerosis.” Nature Reviews\NDisease Primers 3.1 (2017): 1-19.
2. Al-Chalabi, A., & Hardiman, O. “The\Nepidemiology of ALS: a conspiracy of genes,\Nenvironment and time.” Nature Reviews\NNeurology 9.11 (2013): 617-628.
• ⬆ riesgo con el envejecimiento (pico en 60-75 años) (3).
• ♂ > ♀, pero se iguala con la edad avanzada (2).
• Mutaciones en genes específicos (SOD1, C9orf72, etc.) (4).
1. Chio, A., et al. “Global epidemiology of\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis: a systematic\Nreview of the published literature.”\NNeuroepidemiology 41.2 (2013): 118-130.
2. Mehta, P., et al. “Prevalence of amyotrophic lateral\Nsclerosis—United States, 2014.” MMWR Morb Mortal\NWkly Rep 65.8 (2016): 1-12.
3. Logroscino, G., et al. “Incidence of amyotrophic\Nlateral sclerosis in Europe.” J Neurol Neurosurg\NPsychiatry 81.4 (2010): 385-390.
4. Renton, A. E., Chio, A., & Traynor, B. J. “State of play\Nin amyotrophic lateral sclerosis genetics.” Nature\NNeuroscience 17.1 (2014): 17-23.
5. Oskarsson, B., Gendron, T. F., & Staff, N. P.\N“Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis: an update for\N2018.” Mayo Clinic Proceedings. Vol. 93. No.\N11. Elsevier, 2018.
1. Rosen, D. R., et al. “Mutations in Cu/Zn\Nsuperoxide dismutase gene are associated with\Nfamilial amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.” Nature\N362.6415 (1993): 59-62.
2. DeJesus-Hernandez, M., et al. “Expanded GGGGCC\Nhexanucleotide repeat in noncoding region of C9orf72\Ncauses chromosome 9p-linked FTD and ALS.” Neuron\N72.2 (2011): 245-256.
3. Kwiatkowski, T. J., et al. “Mutations in the\NFUS/TLS gene on chromosome 16 cause\Nfamilial amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.” Science\N323.5918 (2009): 1205-1208.
4. Kamel, F., et al. “Lead exposure as a risk factor for\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis.” Neurodegenerative\NDiseases 8.3 (2011): 206-216.
5. Chen, H., et al. “Head injury and amyotrophic\Nlateral sclerosis.” American Journal of\NEpidemiology 166.7 (2007): 810-816.
6. Li, W., et al. “Human endogenous retrovirus-K contributes\Nto motor neuron disease.” Science Translational Medicine 7.\N307 (2015): 307ra153-307ra153.
7. Al-Chalabi, A., et al. “An estimate of amyotrophic\Nlateral sclerosis heritability using twin data.”\NJournal of Neurology, Neurosurgery & Psychiatry\N81.12 (2010): 1324-1326.
• Acumulación de radicales libres ⬆ daño celular (1).
• Inclusiones citoplasmáticas de TDP-43, SOD1, FUS (3).
• Degeneración en el tronco encefálico y médula espinal.
• Activación de caspasas y otros mediadores pro-apoptóticos (5).
• Activación de microglía y astrocitos ➡ liberación de citoquinas proinflamatorias (6).
1. Ferrante, R. J., et al. “Selective sparing of a class of\Nneurons in a transgenic mouse model of ALS.” Journal of\NNeuroscience 17.11 (1997): 4067-4075.
2. Dupuis, L., et al. “Mitochondrial dysfunction in\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis.” Biochimica et\NBiophysica Acta (BBA)-Molecular Basis of\NDisease 1802.1 (2010): 45-51.
3. Neumann, M., et al. “Ubiquitinated TDP-43 in\Nfrontotemporal lobar degeneration and amyotrophic lateral\Nsclerosis.” Science 314.5796 (2006): 130-133.
4. Eisen, A., et al. “Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis:\Na 40-year review.” Journal of Clinical\NNeuroscience 21.6 (2014): 964-970.

5. Martin, L. J. “Neuronal death in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis\Nis apoptosis: possible contribution of a programmed cell death\Nmechanism.” Journal of Neuropathology & Experimental\NNeurology 58.5 (1999): 459-471.
6. Philips, T., & Robberecht, W. “Neuroinflammation\Nin amyotrophic lateral sclerosis: role of glial\Nactivation in motor neuron disease.” The Lancet\NNeurology 10.3 (2011): 253-263.
• Contracciones musculares involuntarias, visibles bajo la piel (2).
• Asociación con demencia frontotemporal (10-15% de los casos) (8).
1. Brooks, B. R. “El Escorial World Federation of\NNeurology criteria for the diagnosis of amyotrophic\Nlateral sclerosis.” Journal of the Neurological\NSciences 124 (1994): 96-107.
2. De Carvalho, M., Dengler, R., & Eisen, A. “The\NAwaji criteria for diagnosis of ALS.” Amyotrophic\NLateral Sclerosis 9.5 (2008): 270-275.
3. Kiernan, M. C., et al. “Amyotrophic\Nlateral sclerosis.” The Lancet 377.\N9769 (2011): 942-955.
4. Brown, R. H., & Al-Chalabi, A. “Amyotrophic\Nlateral sclerosis.” New England Journal of\NMedicine 377.2 (2017): 162-172.
5. Turner, M. R., et al. “Controversies and priorities\Nin amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.” The Lancet\NNeurology 12.3 (2013): 310-322.
6. Swash, M. “Why are upper motor neuron\Nsigns difficult to elicit in amyotrophic lateral\Nsclerosis?.” Journal of the Neurological\NSciences 139 (1996): 10-16.
7. Bensimon, G., et al. “A controlled trial of riluzole in\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis.” New England Journal of\NMedicine 330.9 (1994): 585-591.
8. Lomen-Hoerth, C., et al. “Are amyotrophic\Nlateral sclerosis patients cognitively normal?.”\NNeurology 54.1 (2000): 17-20.
9. Andersen, P. M., et al. “Clinical measures and\Nsurvival prediction in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.”\NJournal of Neurology, Neurosurgery & Psychiatry\N75.5 (2004): 754-760.
• Evidencia de degeneración de motoneuronas\Nsuperiores (UMN) e inferiores (LMN).
• Progresión de los síntomas\Ndentro de una región y a otras\Nregiones del cuerpo (1).
• Evidencia de denervación activa y crónica en al menos tres regiones (2).
• Exclusión de otras patologías (compresión medular, esclerosis múltiple) (3).
• Señal hiperintensa en el tracto corticoespinal (indicativa) (4).
• Identificación de mutaciones conocidas (SOD1, C9orf72, etc.) (5).
• Niveles de CK (creatina quinasa) pueden estar elevados, pero no específicos (6).
• Evaluación de la función de las vías motoras y sensoriales (7).
• Rara vez necesaria, puede mostrar cambios denervativos (8).
1. Brooks, B. R. “El Escorial World Federation of\NNeurology criteria for the diagnosis of amyotrophic\Nlateral sclerosis.” Journal of the Neurological\NSciences 124 (1994): 96-107.
2. De Carvalho, M., Dengler, R., & Eisen, A. “The\NAwaji criteria for diagnosis of ALS.” Amyotrophic\NLateral Sclerosis 9.5 (2008): 270-275.
3. Chio, A., et al. “Neuroimaging in\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis.” Nature\NReviews Neurology 10.11 (2014): 669-678.
4. Turner, M. R., et al. “MRI of the brain in\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis: from technique\Nto pathophysiology.” Nature Reviews\NNeurology 7.11 (2011): 631-639.
5. Renton, A. E., et al. “State of play in\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis genetics.” Nature\NNeuroscience 17.1 (2014): 17-23.
6. Mitchell, J. D., & Borasio, G. D.\N“Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.” The\NLancet 369.9578 (2007): 2031-2041.

7. Vucic, S., & Kiernan, M. C. “Utility of transcranial\Nmagnetic stimulation in delineating amyotrophic lateral\Nsclerosis pathophysiology.” Handbook of Clinical Neurology.\NVol. 116. Elsevier, 2013. 437-447.
8. Swash, M., & Desai, J. “Clinical and\Nneuropathological aspects of ALS.” Advances\Nin Neurology 60 (1993): 15-22.
• Mecanismo: inhibición de la liberación de glutamato (1).
• Efecto: ⬇ progresión de la enfermedad, ⬆ supervivencia (~3 meses) (1).
• Efecto: mejora leve en la función motora en etapas tempranas (2).
• Objetivo: mantener la movilidad y la independencia (3).
• Indicada en casos avanzados con insuficiencia respiratoria (4).
• Control del dolor y otros síntomas (6).
• Apoyo psicológico para paciente y familia (6).
1. Bensimon, G., et al. “A controlled trial of riluzole in\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis.” New England Journal of\NMedicine 330.9 (1994): 585-591.
2. Abe, K., et al. “Effect of edaravone on survival rate of\Npatients with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.” Brain and\NNerve 69.9 (2017): 1033-1044.
3. Dal Bello-Haas, V., & Florence, J. M. “Therapeutic\Nexercise for people with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis or\Nmotor neuron disease.” Cochrane Database of\NSystematic Reviews 5 (2013).

4. Bourke, S. C., et al. “Effects of non-invasive ventilation on\Nsurvival and quality of life in patients with amyotrophic lateral\Nsclerosis: a randomised controlled trial.” The Lancet\NNeurology 5.2 (2006): 140-147.
5. Heffernan, C., et al. “Nutritional management in\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis: a review.” Amyotrophic\NLateral Sclerosis 5.2 (2004): 72-83.
6. Oliver, D., et al. “Palliative care in amyotrophic\Nlateral sclerosis: a review.” Amyotrophic Lateral\NSclerosis 10.1 (2009): 27-39.
• Sintomas iniciales (debilidad muscular asimétrica) ➡ progresión a otros grupos musculares (1).
• Fase intermedia: Afectación bilateral, disfagia y disartria.
• Fase avanzada: Dependencia total, insuficiencia respiratoria (2).
• Sobrevida media: 3-5 años desde el diagnóstico (3).
• ELA bulbar: ⬇ supervivencia debido a complicaciones respiratorias y nutricionales (5).
• Uso de Riluzol y Edaravone ➡ ⬆ supervivencia (6).
• Ventilación no invasiva (VNI) ➡ ⬆ calidad de vida y ⬆ supervivencia (7).
• Dificultad para deglutir y ⬇ ingesta calórica (9).
1. Brooks, B. R., et al. “El Escorial World Federation\Nof Neurology criteria for the diagnosis of amyotrophic\Nlateral sclerosis.” Journal of the Neurological\NSciences 124 (1994): 96-107.
2. Swash, M. “Why are upper motor neuron\Nsigns difficult to elicit in amyotrophic lateral\Nsclerosis?.” Journal of the Neurological\NSciences 139 (1996): 10-16.
3. Chiò, A., et al. “Prognostic factors in ALS: A\Ncritical review.” Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis\N10.5-6 (2009): 310-323.
4. Gordon, P. H., et al. “Prognostic factors\Nin ALS: a population-based study.”\NNeurology 60.5 (2003): 813-819.

5. Traynor, B. J., et al. “Effect of a multidisciplinary amyotrophic\Nlateral sclerosis (ALS) clinic on ALS survival: a population based\Nstudy, 1996–2000.” Journal of Neurology, Neurosurgery &\NPsychiatry 74.9 (2003): 1258-1261.

6. Miller, R. G., et al. “Practice parameter update: The care of\Nthe patient with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis: drug, nutritional,\Nand respiratory therapies (an evidence-based review).”\NNeurology 73.15 (2009): 1218-1226.

7. Bourke, S. C., et al. “Effects of non-invasive ventilation on\Nsurvival and quality of life in patients with amyotrophic lateral\Nsclerosis: a randomised controlled trial.” The Lancet\NNeurology 5.2 (2006): 140-147.
8. Heffernan, C., et al. “Nutritional management in\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis: a review.” Amyotrophic\NLateral Sclerosis 5.2 (2004): 72-83.
9. Stambler, N., et al. “Analysis of survival in\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis using a prospective\Nmulticenter natural history database.” Neurology\N47.2 (1996): 583-590.
• Combinación de Riluzol con otros fármacos (3).
• Objetivo: corregir mutaciones específicas (ej. SOD1, C9orf72) (4).
• Métodos: edición génica CRISPR/Cas9, oligonucleótidos antisentido (5).
• Tecnología asistida (AAC) para comunicación no verbal (8).
• Mejora en dispositivos de ventilación no invasiva (9).
• Dietas específicas para mantener el estado nutricional óptimo (10).
1. Petrov, D., et al. “ALS clinical trials review: 20\Nyears of failure. Are we any closer to registering\Na new treatment?.” Frontiers in Aging\NNeuroscience 9 (2017): 68.
2. Benatar, M., et al. “Randomized,\Ndouble-blind, placebo-controlled trial of\Narimoclomol in rapidly progressive SOD1 ALS.\N” Neurology 89.4 (2017): 381-390.
3. Paganoni, S., et al. “Trial of sodium\Nphenylbutyrate–taurursodiol for amyotrophic\Nlateral sclerosis.” New England Journal of\NMedicine 383.10 (2020): 919-930.
4. Gaj, T., et al. “ZFN, TALEN, and\NCRISPR/Cas-based methods for genome engineering.\N” Trends in Biotechnology 31.7 (2013): 397-405.
5. Miller, T. M., et al. “Phase 1–2 trial of antisense\Noligonucleotide tofersen for SOD1 ALS.” New England\NJournal of Medicine 383.2 (2020): 109-119.
6. Berry, J. D., et al. “Cell-based therapies for\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis.” Experimental\NNeurology 262 (2014): 91-104.
7. Kondo, T., & Tsuji, S. “Stem cell therapy for\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis: an update of\Nrecent clinical trials.” Journal of Clinical\NMedicine 3.1 (2014): 197-204.
8. Beukelman, D. R., & Mirenda, P. “Augmentative and\Nalternative communication: Supporting children and adults\Nwith complex communication needs.” Paul H. Brookes\NPublishing Co., Inc., 2013.
9. Lechtzin, N., et al. “Home noninvasive ventilation for\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis: outcomes and costs.”\NAmyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis and Frontotemporal\NDegeneration 17.5-6 (2016): 453-461.

10. Pupillo, E., et al. “Nutritional management of patients with\Namyotrophic lateral sclerosis in Europe: results of a survey.”\NAmyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis and Frontotemporal\NDegeneration 14.5-6 (2013): 297-303.
